Views

class NewsApp.views.APILoginView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ObtainAuthToken

Return an auth token and basic user info on successful login.

Returns:

JSON with token, user_id, username, and role.

post(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]
class NewsApp.views.APILogoutView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Delete the current user’s auth token, effectively logging them out.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
post(request)[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleApproveAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Approve an article (Editors only).

Parameters:

pk – Primary key of the Article to approve.

Returns:

Serialized article data with HTTP 200.

permission_classes = [<class 'NewsApp.views.IsEditor'>]
post(request, pk)[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleCreateView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, CreateView

Create a new article (Journalists only).

form_class

alias of ArticleForm

form_valid(form)[source]

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

model

alias of Article

success_url = '/articles/'
template_name = 'articles/article_write.html'
test_func()[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleDeleteView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, DeleteView

Delete an article. Journalists may only delete their own.

delete(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Call the delete() method on the fetched object and then redirect to the success URL.

model

alias of Article

success_url = '/articles/'
template_name = 'articles/article_delete.html'
test_func()[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Retrieve, update, or delete a single article.

  • GET — Visible per role (Readers: approved only).

  • PUT — Editors or the article’s own Journalist.

  • DELETE — Editors or the article’s own Journalist.

delete(request, pk)[source]
get(request, pk)[source]
put(request, pk)[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleDetailView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, DetailView

Display a single article, enforcing role-based visibility.

context_object_name = 'article'
model

alias of Article

template_name = 'articles/article_detail.html'
test_func()[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleListCreateAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

List articles or create a new one.

  • GET — Returns articles filtered by the caller’s role.

  • POST — Creates an article; Journalists only.

get(request)[source]
get_permissions()[source]

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

post(request)[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleListView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, ListView

List articles filtered by the current user’s role.

context_object_name = 'articles'
get_queryset()[source]

Return the list of items for this view.

The return value must be an iterable and may be an instance of QuerySet in which case QuerySet specific behavior will be enabled.

model

alias of Article

template_name = 'articles/article_list.html'
test_func()[source]
class NewsApp.views.ArticleUpdateView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, UserPassesTestMixin, UpdateView

Edit an existing article. Journalists may only edit their own.

form_class

alias of ArticleForm

form_valid(form)[source]

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

get_success_url()[source]

Return the URL to redirect to after processing a valid form.

model

alias of Article

template_name = 'articles/article_write.html'
test_func()[source]
class NewsApp.views.IsAuthenticatedReader[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to any authenticated user.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class NewsApp.views.IsEditor[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to Editors only.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class NewsApp.views.IsEditorOrJournalist[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow Editors full access; restrict Journalists to their own objects.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class NewsApp.views.IsJournalist[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to Journalists only.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class NewsApp.views.LoginView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: FormView

Authenticate a user and redirect to the article list.

dispatch(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]
form_class

alias of CustomLoginForm

form_valid(form)[source]

If the form is valid, redirect to the supplied URL.

success_url = '/articles/'
template_name = 'registration/login.html'
class NewsApp.views.LogoutView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: View

Log out the current user and redirect to login.

get(request)[source]

class NewsApp.views.SignupView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: CreateView

Register a new user and redirect to login on success.

dispatch(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]
form_class

alias of CustomUserCreationForm

form_valid(form)[source]

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

model

alias of CustomUser

success_url = '/'
template_name = 'registration/signup.html'
class NewsApp.views.SubscribedArticlesAPIView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Return approved articles from the user’s subscribed publishers and journalists.

get(request)[source]
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
NewsApp.views.approve_article(request, pk)[source]

Approve an article for publishing and notify the author by email.

Parameters:

  • request – The HTTP request (must be POST to approve).

  • pk – Primary key of the Article to approve.

Returns:

Redirect to the article detail page on success, or the approval form.

NewsApp.views.can_approve_articles(user)[source]

Return True if user may approve articles (Editor only).

NewsApp.views.can_create_content(user)[source]

Return True if user may create content (Journalist only).

NewsApp.views.can_delete_content(user)[source]

Return True if user may delete content (Editor or Journalist).

NewsApp.views.can_edit_content(user)[source]

Return True if user may edit content (Editor or Journalist).

NewsApp.views.can_view_content(user)[source]

Return True if user holds any content-viewing role (Reader, Editor, Journalist).

NewsApp.views.is_editor(user)[source]

Return True if user is authenticated with the Editor role.

NewsApp.views.is_journalist(user)[source]

Return True if user is authenticated with the Journalist role.

NewsApp.views.is_reader(user)[source]

Return ‘True’ if user is authenticated and has role ‘Reader’

NewsApp.views.profile_view(request)[source]

Display the current user’s profile.

Parameters:

request – The HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered profile, or redirects to article write.

NewsApp.views.reader_subscriptions(request)[source]

Display the current user’s subscribed publishers and journalists.

Parameters:

request – The HTTP request.

Returns:

Rendered subscriptions page.

NewsApp.views.subscribe_to_journalist(request, journalist_id)[source]

Subscribe the current user to a journalist.

Parameters:

journalist_id – PK of the journalist (CustomUser) to subscribe to.

NewsApp.views.subscribe_to_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]

Subscribe the current user to a publisher.

Parameters:

publisher_id – PK of the Publisher to subscribe to.

NewsApp.views.unsubscribe_from_journalist(request, journalist_id)[source]

Allow readers to unsubscribe from a journalist.

Parameters:

journalist_id – PK of the journalist (CustomUser) to unsubscribe from.

NewsApp.views.unsubscribe_from_publisher(request, publisher_id)[source]

Unsubscribe the current user from a publisher.

Parameters:

publisher_id – PK of the Publisher to unsubscribe from.
